page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon architecture provides hardware-level support for advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA-NI. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates mid-range performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yielded a score of 1842.09, while the multi-core test reached 7750.38. Cryptographic capabilities are highlighted by a PassMark CPU encryption score of 7300.05, and the PassMark CPU extended instructions test scored 6963.53.

Memory performance is characterized by a high cached read bandwidth of 421.06 GB/sec for small blocks, which drops to 58.32 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 41.33 GB/sec. The instance achieved a PassMark database operations score of 3977.92 and processed 7.83 million Redis SET operations per second. Static web serving throughput reached 11.46 GB/sec.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.c7nex.2xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for web serving, batch processing, Redis caching layers, and applications utilizing vector or cryptographic instructions.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Its x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions like AES and SHA-NI. Benchmark results place the instance in the average performance tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7750.38, a Redis SET throughput of 7.83 million operations per second, and a static web serving throughput of 11.46 GB/sec. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents a resource-dense option for compute-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for web serving, caching layers, and cryptographic or vector-based processing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput web serving and batch processing.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7nex.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU, 16 GB RAM compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and 10 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors, advanced vector instructions, and high-throughput network performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8369B processor
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 compute-to-RAM ratio
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction support
Optimized for web serving, Redis caching, and batch processing workloads
